mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic strength and rising corporate earnings potential, leading to positive equity returns over the following month. This is an always-on, pro-cyclical signal.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery in employment has coincided with a bull market.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.5 percentage points over 6 months indicates a significant labor market deterioration, signaling an impending recession and causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This implies future aggressive tightening, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary environment of the 1970s was negative for equities.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to generate a negative Information Coefficient when tested on out-of-sample data from a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) growth is particularly damaging for equities during a fragile economic recovery, as it signals a failure to regain pre-crisis momentum and raises the risk of a double-dip recession.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2010-2011 recovery has been characterized by intermittent growth scares.
failure_condition: The signal has a positive hit rate (predicts negative returns which are actually positive) greater than 60% during post-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in month-over-month retail sales (RSAFS) growth indicates stronger-than-expected consumer demand, leading to immediate upward revisions in near-term earnings forecasts and positive equity returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between monthly RSAFS changes and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) environment (FEDFUNDS near zero), any acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL) forces markets to price in a sooner-than-expected policy normalization, which is negative for risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Dec 2011, the Fed has maintained ZIRP for three years.
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the correlation between monthly CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broad-based economic slowdown due to its strong forward linkages to construction, manufacturing, and consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of HOUST falls > 15% year-over-year
evidence_refs: fact:The 2006-2007 collapse in housing preceded the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive average 60-day forward return in the 12 months following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: Positive year-over-year growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing and industrial capacity, which is directly tied to the earnings of a large portion of the S&P 500, thus driving positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Conference Board Leading Economic Index.
failure_condition: The beta of S&P 500 20-day returns to the year-over-year change in INDPRO is negative or statistically insignificant over any 5-year period.
